setting.note: Not the medieval alleys of Islamic Cairo but the European-built downtown of the 1930s, Wust el-Balad: Art Deco blocks, cafes and bars around Talaat Harb and Kasr El Nil, a whole society stacked inside one apartment house from the ground-floor offices to the iron huts on the roof. The map keeps to a few downtown streets, all real, all within a short walk of Talaat Harb Square.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Alaa Al Aswany, first published in Arabic in 2002. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Yacoubian Building, Alaa Al Aswany (2002). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The burnt ochre of downtown Cairo brick at dusk, the colour of the Art Deco facades along Talaat Harb. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
